title: [Bug]: Error: "cannot unmarshal object into Go struct field MachineVM.IgnitionFilePath of type string"
body:
### Issue Description

When running the Podman machine commands, I consistently get the same error:

```cmd
$ podman machine list
Error: error listing vms: json: cannot unmarshal object into Go struct field MachineVM.IgnitionFilePath of type string
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue:
1. Initialize the Podman machine using Podman-desktop 0.10.0 on macOS Ventura
2. Run `podman machine list` on the CLI

I have tried completely uninstalling Podman and Podman Desktop and re-installing.

### Describe the results you received

```cmd
$ podman machine list
Error: error listing vms: json: cannot unmarshal object into Go struct field MachineVM.IgnitionFilePath of type string
```

### Describe the results you expected

Expected to see a list of Podman machines.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

When trying to use just the Podman CLI, I get an "Error: unable to start host networking: "could not find \"gvproxy\"".  So tried using Podman desktop instead.  Podman itself works OK, and I can run containers from the CLI.

comments.body: Possibly corrupted config file, please provide the output of `cat ~/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default.json` and check if you have other config files in this directory.

On your mac, that file will be in a different location.

And if you are missing gvproxy, that is also a problem.  From where did you get your podman install for the mac?

I installed using `brew install podman`.

Here's the config file:


There's also a `.ign` file in that directory:

```cmd
$ ls ~/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/ -l
.rw-r--r--@ 6.7k arthurbarr 17 Jan 14:05 podman-machine-default.ign
.rw-r--r--@ 2.9k arthurbarr 17 Jan 14:06 podman-machine-default.json
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@baude @afbjorklund What should we do with this one?

I guess you would have to add some kind of versioning, with corresponding upgrade functionality,
if you want the config to be portable between different versions with different implementation ?

I do not understand how they end up in this situation? The code already has such functionality to handle schema changes. Of course their could be a bug in there.

Is it possible hat the podman version in podman-desktop is different form the one on the cli?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we heard no further feedback, closing.
